method: wait for target camera, inject /data= historical date, wait 500ms and verify selected date, wait 3500ms more for render settle, crop once
observedErrors: A 500ms post-injection crop can capture the Google Earth splash screen even when URL/date are correct.
Using screenshot byte size as a blank/splash detector misses high-byte splash images.
When reusing a page, camera movement alone can accept stale coordinates; coordinate searches should require proximity to the requested lat/lon.
Treating the current camera as stale rejects a valid same-location target; use target proximity first, stale-movement only for non-coordinate searches.
Date/place-correct crops can still save blurry low-resolution tiles; require a minimum detail score before accepting the crop.
Decoding URL date immediately after commit can hit an execution-context-destroyed race; wait about 500ms or retry briefly.
